in_scope: FRAME-RAIL milling and dado for cord-stop pin
FRAME-GUSSET cutting from 1/2 inch Baltic birch plywood
out_of_scope: Bar blanks — bandsaw + plane + manual end-shave for tuning
Resonator tubes — deferred to v2 build

datum_strategy: Lower-left corner of stock; Z zero on top of stock surface; uniform across all three operation groups
workholding: Vacuum table for plywood gussets; toggle clamps and L-fences for hard-maple rails and legs
release_checks: Verify stock thickness with calipers at 4 corners before any Z-axis move
Dry-run program at Z+0.5 in before first cut on each new operation group
Confirm bit length and stickout on T01 toolpath; the rail thickness is 1.250 in
After first part of each batch: pause, measure critical dimensions, accept or revise feeds
Vacuum hold-down: confirm seal before plywood operations; expect to lose seal on through-cuts

operation_groups.operation: outline both rails to 21.0 x 2.25 x 1.25 in
0.125 in dado along the inside top edge of each rail at 0.250 in depth for cord-stop seating
two 1.0 x 0.5 x 0.5 in mortises in the top face of each leg, spaced to receive the gusset
cut 4 gussets at 6.0 x 6.0 x 0.5 in, 45-degree triangle, hypotenuse outboard

pre_cam_assumptions: Stock thickness arrives within +/- 1/64 in of nominal
Hard maple is straight-grained and free of knots in the cutter path
Plywood is B/BB or better; no voids in the cutter path
Vacuum table is functional and can hold a 12x12 plywood blank
post_cnc_handoff: All four operation groups produce raw-cut parts; sand to 220 grit and break edges 1/16 in before frame glue-up (assembly-manual.md Step 8 onwards)
